summary: Replacement simulation_engineer reviewed and continued the preserved uncommitted M6 diplomacy/legitimacy implementation, fixed save/load M6 slice preservation and a lint issue, and is routing the deterministic diplomacy and legitimacy substrate for systems_architect review.

tests_run.result: Reviewed dirty worktree; implementation changes are in allowed package/test/project-message paths. Untracked ui-samples/ remains unrelated and untouched.

24 test files passed, 177 tests passed, including tests/sim-core-m6-diplomacy-legitimacy.test.ts for command parity, stable ordering, no recognition cycles, legitimacy source conservation/reason codes, and save malformed/missing/future-version rejection paths.
2 test files passed, 3 tests passed, including tests/sim-ai-m6-diplomacy-ai.test.ts for public-query AI planning and same GameCommand submission path.
1 test file passed, 10 tests passed, including M6 diplomacy and legitimacy command/query schema acceptance and malformed sourceKind rejection.
2 test files passed, 16 tests passed, including save envelope and save/load contract coverage after M6 save-format parser changes.
Node/Worker-compatible hashes matched for hello, command/query, save/load, M4 replay, and M5 playable loop canaries.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism canaries matched; root Vitest passed 37 files / 257 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file / 4 tests.
ESLint passed after removing an unused assignment in the M6 property test.
No whitespace errors after REVIEW status and handoff write.

decisions: Preserved the previous writer's allowed-path implementation instead of reverting it.
Kept diplomacy and legitimacy authoritative in sim-core WorldState m6 state with explicit schemaVersion 1, branded IDs, reason-coded events and deterministic canonical ordering.
Kept player and AI on the same GameCommand paths: sim.propose-diplomatic-agreement, sim.answer-diplomatic-agreement, and sim.record-legitimacy-source.
Fixed save envelope copying so state.m6 is preserved through createSaveEnvelopeV1/copySaveBody, and added M6 required-slice rejection in loadSaveV1 when the runtime expects M6 state.
Did not touch PROJECT_STATUS.md, ui-samples/, UI packages, content-source, tools, docs gate files, or manual node battle scope.
risks: R4 shared-surface change touches sim-core command/query/world-state, protocol schemas, save-format, and sim-ai; systems_architect review is required before acceptance.
M6 diplomacy is intentionally bounded substrate work, not full peace treaties, grand-strategy manager, server/multiplayer, telemetry, arbitrary-code scripting, or manual node battle.
Protocol/save changes are additive schema v1 surfaces but still need architecture scrutiny for long-term compatibility and replay semantics.
Untracked ui-samples/ remains present in the worktree and was not inspected or modified.
requested_action: Review the M6 diplomacy/legitimacy substrate for architecture boundaries, deterministic authority, command parity, save/protocol compatibility, and R4 scope discipline. If accepted, route onward per project review flow.
